entities.name: MIG
entities.description:
A welding process supported by the welder (MIG).
Metal Inert Gas welding process specifications shown for 120V and 240V configurations.
Metal Inert Gas welding mode of the machine; supports wire feeding and spool gun option for aluminum.
Specific process selection value displayed under Process (MIG Steel).
Metal Inert Gas welding process referenced where certain causes (shielding gas issues) apply.
Welding configuration where ground clamp to negative terminal, wire feed power to positive terminal, MIG gun to wire feed, and wire feed control connected inside welder.
Procedure that details connecting ground clamp to negative terminal, wire feed power to positive terminal, and connecting MIG gun and wire feed control.
Metal Inert Gas welding process shown as a column in the selector chart.
MIG welding process configuration: Ground Clamp negative, Wire Feed Power positive, wire feed connected to (+), DCEP.
A welding process option listed in the selection chart (metal inert gas welding).
A welding process using solid wire and shielding gas; fast production, easy to learn, clean welds with no slag, better control on thin materials.

entities.name: Flux-Cored
entities.description:
A welding process supported by the welder (flux-cored).
Specification indicating whether the wire is solid core or flux‑cored; determines appropriate Feed Roller selection.
Type of welding wire compatible with the knurled side of the feed roller.
Type of welding wire that requires lower tension and can be crushed by excessive force.
Consumable wire used in flux-cored welding that does not require external shielding gas.
Type of welding wire that contains flux and can be used without shielding gas.
Self-shielded flux-cored welding wire that does not use external shielding gas.
A welding process represented as one of four options in the selector chart.
A welding process option listed in the selection chart (flux-cored arc welding).
A welding process using cored wire and typically no external shielding gas; good for outdoor/windy conditions, forgiving on rusty/dirty steels, good out-of-position capability, high deposition rates.

entities.name: TIG
entities.description:
A welding process supported by the welder (TIG).
Tungsten Inert Gas welding process specifications shown for 120V and 240V configurations.
Tungsten Inert Gas welding mode; supports welding of stainless steel, mild steel, and chrome moly (chrome moly TIG-only).
Welding process selection displayed on the LCD; TIG is selected via the Main Control Knob.
The TIG welding mode selected on the welder's control panel to perform TIG welding operations.
Operating mode for TIG welding where torch is negative, wire feed power is disconnected, and gas is connected.
Tungsten Inert Gas welding process shown as a column in the selector chart.
TIG welding process configuration: Ground Clamp positive, TIG Torch negative, wire feed power disconnected, DCEN.
A welding process option listed in the selection chart (tungsten inert gas welding).
A welding process that produces the highest quality, aesthetic welds with precise control and usable on a variety of materials.

entities.name: Stick
entities.description:
A welding process supported by the welder (stick).
Shielded metal arc welding mode; TIG/Stick table notes that aluminum and chrome moly are not listed for Stick.
Welding process mode 'Stick' shown on the LCD screen.
Process selection readout indicating welding process type (Stick).
A welding process (shielded metal arc welding) that produces slag covering the weld.
Type of weld that will have a coat of slag over it until cleaned.
Shielded metal arc welding process using an electrode (stick) to create welds; context for spatter and burn-through issues.
Consumable filler rod used in stick welding to carry the arc and provide filler metal.
Welding mode where the electrode holder is connected to the positive terminal and the ground clamp to the negative terminal; wire feed power disconnected.
Stick welding process shown as a column in the selector chart.
Holder for stick welding electrode; in table shown connected as positive for Stick process.
Stick welding process configuration: Ground Clamp negative, Electrode Holder positive, wire feed power disconnected, DCEP.
A welding process option listed in the selection chart (stick/arc welding).
A welding process suitable for outdoor/windy conditions, forgiving on rusty/dirty steels, provides deep penetration and good for thicker materials.

entities.name: Eye protection
entities.description: Protective eyewear required for welding; referenced with a minimum shade level.

entities.name: Exposure limits
entities.description: General requirement to follow exposure limits (OSHA PELs or ACGIH TLVs) for hazardous substances.

entities.name: Confined space
entities.description: Work environment condition cited where ventilation or air-supplied respirator is required.

entities.name: Footwear
entities.description: Requirement category for shoes/boots specifying leather and fire-resistant materials.

entities.name: metal wall
entities.description: A metal structural element such as a wall or ceiling on which welding or cutting may be performed.

entities.name: combustibles
entities.description:
Materials that can ignite or burn if exposed to heat, sparks, or slag during cutting or welding.
Items that can ignite from arc or sparks; must be cleared or protected within 35 feet (10 meters).

entities.name: fire watch
entities.description: A person designated to monitor for fire during and after cutting or welding operations, equipped with a fire extinguisher.

entities.name: fire extinguisher
entities.description:
Portable device used by the fire watch to extinguish fires.
Recommended type of fire extinguisher to keep near the work area and know how to use.

entities.name: combustible coating
entities.description: A surface coating that is combustible and presents a fire hazard when welding or cutting.

entities.name: combustible internal structure
entities.description: Internal materials within assemblies (e.g., inside walls or ceilings) that are combustible.

entities.name: hot slag
entities.description:
Molten waste material produced during welding or cutting operations that can cause ignition if disposed into combustible-containing containers.
Molten or solidified residue from welding that, along with arc, can cause fire.
Molten residue from welding that can damage cylinders if it contacts them.
Residue produced on a weld that should be removed before examining the weld appearance.
Residue covering welds produced by flux-cored or stick welding that must be removed.
Residue produced in some welding processes that can protect welds; not needed for gas-shielded MIG welds.
Byproduct of flux-cored wire welding that shields the weld from impurities and covers the bead until removed
Coating of slag over stick welds until cleaned.
Solidified layer of flux and impurities on top of a stick weld that shields the weld from impurities.

entities.name: container that held unknown substance
entities.description: Containers that previously held unknown or combustible substances which may produce flammable or explosive vapors when heated.

entities.name: venting
entities.description: Opening or providing an outlet for enclosed containers (including castings) before preheating, welding, or cutting to release vapors.

entities.name: clean and purge containers
entities.description: Removing residues and purging containers to eliminate combustible or unknown substances before applying heat.

entities.name: move combustibles to safe location
entities.description: Physically relocating combustible materials away from the metal surface being worked on to prevent ignition.

entities.name: designate fire watch for 30 minutes
aliases: fire watch for at least one half hour
entities.description: Assigning someone to monitor for fire equipped with an extinguisher during cutting and for at least 30 minutes after completion.

entities.name: do not weld or cut on combustible-coated material
entities.description: Instruction to avoid welding or cutting on materials that have combustible coatings or internal combustible structures unless an approved hazard-elimination method is used.

entities.name: examine for evidence of fire
entities.description: Conducting a thorough inspection after welding to look for signs of fire, noting that visible smoke or flame may be delayed.

entities.name: hidden smoldering ignition
entities.description: A fire that starts without immediate visible smoke or flame and may appear some time after welding has been completed.

entities.name: arc
entities.description:
The electric discharge produced during welding that can cause fire and slag.
Electrical discharge between electrode and workpiece that melts metal to form the weld; light can cause eye/skin damage and fumes should not be inhaled.
Distance between the electrode tip and the workpiece, affecting arc stability and weld shape.
Distance between electrode and workpiece; affects heat input and spatter
Electric arc between electrode and workpiece that creates the weld puddle

The item being welded; users must insulate themselves from the workpiece and ground.
The metal parts being welded together; their edges may need chamfering or beveling for proper weld penetration.
The metal object being welded; location where ground clamp should attach near the weld area.
The metal surface being welded.
A level surface that can bear the Welder's weight and is near the work area.
The metal part(s) being welded; may require chamfering or beveling on thicker edges to allow weld penetration.
The metal being welded; ground clamp must be attached to bare metal near the weld area.
The metal piece being welded.
The metal item being welded (the surface tapped or stroked to ignite the arc).
The piece being welded; electrode is lifted from it when finished.
Sample piece being welded and tested in the strike test illustration.
The metal being welded into which penetration occurs
The material being welded that can overheat and melt away causing burn-through.
The metal being welded; shown as flat workpiece in illustrations.
The parent metal being welded into which the weld penetrates.
The metal piece being welded
The metal pieces being welded; must be cleaned to bare metal for good welds and clamped securely.
Metal pieces being welded together
The metal piece being welded; may require cleaning to bare metal to prevent spatter.
Metal item being welded; cleanliness and contact with ground affect weld quality.

Electrical supply or outlet used to energize the welder.

Clamp that contacts the workpiece to provide the welding ground connection.

entities.name: pacemaker
entities.description: Medical device carried by some people; electromagnetic fields near welding equipment can interfere with or cause failure of pacemakers.

entities.name: water ingress
entities.description: Presence of water entering the welder or working in wet conditions that increases risk of electric shock.

entities.name: electromagnetic interference
entities.description: Interference from electromagnetic fields that can affect pacemakers.

entities.name: disconnect power and discharge electrode
entities.description: Action: turn off and disconnect power and discharge electrode to ground before setting down torch/electrode holder and before service.

entities.name: avoid touching energized parts
aliases: do not touch energized electrical parts
entities.description: Action: do not touch energized parts; wear dry insulating gloves; do not touch electrode holder, electrode, torch, or welding wire with bare hands.

entities.name: use grounded GFCI
aliases: connect to grounded GFCI-protected power supply only

Action to lower welding current to reduce workpiece overheating and prevent burn-through.
Decrease welding current to reduce heat input.

entities.name: weld spatter
entities.description: Large, irregular dots/particles scattered around the weld bead and weld pool area indicating excessive spatter.

entities.name: crooked/wavy bead
entities.description:
Weld bead that is irregular, wavy, or crooked rather than straight; overlapping misaligned bead shapes.
Weld bead that curves and wanders laterally instead of running straight along the joint.

entities.name: welding wire contamination
entities.description: Welding wire contaminated with oil, coatings, or residues.

entities.name: nozzle cleanliness
entities.description: Cleanliness of the welding nozzle; buildup affects shielding and spatter.

entities.name: operator steadiness
entities.description: Operator control / steadiness when welding; using two hands or bracing reduces bead wander.

entities.name: clean wire task
entities.description: Task: make certain welding wire is clean and free from oil, coatings, and residues.

entities.name: check polarity task
entities.description: Task: check that polarity is set correctly for the type of welding.

entities.name: increase gas flow task
entities.description: Task: increase shielding gas flow in MIG welding to prevent spatter.

entities.name: use two hands task
entities.description: Task: use two hands or rest hand on a steady surface to improve welding accuracy.

entities.name: clean welds
entities.description: Action of removing slag from stick welds before diagnosis.

entities.name: weld diagnosis table
entities.description: Table showing cross-section diagram, photo of weld bead, problem label, and correction instruction for diagnosing weld issues.

entities.name: cross-section diagram
entities.description:
Small cross-section illustration used in the weld diagnosis table to show weld profiles.
The single exploded-view drawing on the page showing relative positions of numbered components for reassembly and parts ordering.
Visual illustration dividing the 10-minute period to show ON/OFF ratio for the duty cycle example.

entities.name: weld bead photograph
entities.description: Photograph of the actual weld bead shown in the weld diagnosis table.

entities.name: problem label
entities.description: Label identifying the weld problem shown in the table.

entities.name: clean welds first notice
entities.description: Prominent instruction to clean welds first before diagnosis.

entities.name: coat of slag
entities.description: Phrase describing the layer of slag that covers stick welds until cleaned.

entities.name: small cross-section diagram (top)
entities.description: Specifies that the cross-section diagram is positioned at the top of each table column.

entities.name: photograph of the actual weld bead (middle)
entities.description: Specifies that the photo of the weld bead is positioned in the middle of each table column.

entities.name: label identifying the problem (bottom)
entities.description: Specifies that the problem label is positioned at the bottom of each table column.

entities.name: Weld penetration too shallow
entities.description: Condition where weld does not penetrate sufficiently into the workpiece; implied by 'too fast' travel or too long/short arc.

entities.name: Raise electrode (increase arc length)
entities.description: Action to move the electrode away from the workpiece to increase arc length when arc is too short.

entities.name: Lower electrode (decrease arc length)
entities.description: Action to move the electrode closer to the workpiece to decrease arc length when arc is too long.

entities.name: Weld bead profile
entities.description: Cross-sectional shape of the weld bead (as shown in three profile views to indicate conditions).

entities.name: Electrode drag distance
entities.description: How far the electrode is dragged over the weld pool during the drag technique (implied by side-view drag illustrations).

entities.name: notch below bead
entities.description: A narrow, shallow depression below the weld bead indicating inadequate penetration.

entities.name: cavity/crater
entities.description: A large void extending below the base metal surface caused by excessive penetration or burn-through.

entities.name: bead profile: wide and flat
entities.description:
Bead shape raised above surface and wide/flat, associated with inadequate penetration.
A bead profile that is wide and flat, may indicate excessive penetration or undercut

entities.name: bead profile: full and slightly raised
entities.description: Bead shape that is full and slightly raised, associated with proper penetration.

entities.name: bead profile: excessive melt-through
entities.description: Bead shape showing excessive melt-through associated with burn-through/excess penetration.

entities.name: decrease current
entities.description:
Adjust the welding machine to a lower amperage to reduce workpiece heat and penetration for thinner workpieces.
Adjust the welding current downward to correct high-current bead defects

entities.name: Heat Scale diagram
entities.description: A horizontal arrow diagram showing the relative heat input range labeled 'Not hot enough', 'Ideal heat', and 'Too hot'.

entities.name: thicker workpieces
entities.description:
Workpieces with greater material thickness that require increased heat/penetration to weld properly.
Workpieces that are too thick or positioned too near each other causing welding problems

entities.name: ripple pattern
entities.description: Surface texture pattern of the weld bead indicating consistency

entities.name: Current Too Low
entities.description: Fault condition where welding current is insufficient causing narrow raised bead and poor fusion/penetration

entities.name: Current Too High
entities.description: Fault condition where welding current is excessive causing wide flat bead and undercut at edges

entities.name: Weld Speed Too Fast
entities.description: Fault where travel speed is excessive causing narrow, irregular bead with poor fusion

entities.name: Weld Speed Too Slow
entities.description: Fault where travel speed is too slow causing wide, heavy convex overlapping bead

entities.name: Arc Length Too Short
entities.description: Fault where electrode is held too close causing narrow, irregular bead and possible contact

entities.name: Arc Length Too Long
entities.description: Fault where electrode is held too far causing irregular, porous or spattered bead with porosity

entities.name: Increase distance
entities.description: Increase arc length by raising the electrode to correct too-short-arc defects

entities.name: Decrease distance
entities.description: Decrease arc length by lowering the electrode to correct too-long-arc defects

entities.name: undercut
entities.description: Groove melted into the base metal adjacent to the weld toe reducing cross-section

entities.name: high-crowned bead
entities.description: Raised, convex bead profile indicating insufficient fusion

entities.name: overlap
entities.description: Excess weld metal overlapping base metal without proper fusion

entities.name: narrow raised bead
entities.description: A bead profile that is narrow and raised, indicating insufficient fusion/penetration

entities.name: narrow irregular bead
entities.description: A thin, uneven bead with poor fusion and sparse ripples

entities.name: wide heavy convex overlapping bead
entities.description: A wide, heavy, convex bead showing overlapping weld metal buildup

entities.name: spatter
entities.description: Small droplets of weld metal expelled around the bead, contributing to rough appearance

entities.name: poor tie-in
entities.description: Lack of proper fusion at the bead edges where the weld joins the base metal

entities.name: Welding speed too slow
entities.description: Welding travel speed that is too low, allowing excessive heat input.

entities.name: Increase welding speed
entities.description: Increase and keep steady the welding travel speed to reduce heat per unit length.

entities.name: Hold torch at proper angles
entities.description: Instruction to maintain correct torch angles for proper weld penetration.

entities.name: Slow down
entities.description: Decrease welding speed so fill material has time to melt into the weld location.

entities.name: Weld bead droops below the workpiece surface
entities.description: Visual observation: weld bead droops below the workpiece surface; dark oval shape hangs down through the joint.

entities.name: Weld bead visible underneath
entities.description: Visual characteristic of proper penetration where the weld bead is visible underneath the workpiece.

entities.name: Weld bead sits only on the surface
entities.description: Visual characteristic of inadequate penetration where the weld bead does not penetrate and remains on the surface.

entities.name: Improper clamping
entities.description: Workpieces not clamped securely, leading to weld defects (listed as a possible cause).

entities.name: Inconsistent welding speed
entities.description:
Variation in travel speed during welding that can cause porosity and bead irregularities.
Uneven travel speed during welding that can affect bead quality and appearance.

entities.name: Make tack welds
entities.description: Procedure to make small welds that hold workpieces in place before final welding.

entities.name: Clamp workpieces securely
entities.description: Action to clamp the workpieces firmly to prevent movement during welding.

entities.name: Clean fill material and Electrode
entities.description: Task to ensure filler materials and electrode are free from oil, coatings, and residues.

entities.name: Maintain steady weld speed
entities.description: Action to keep a consistent welding travel speed to avoid defects like porosity.

entities.name: Weld small portion and allow to cool
entities.description: Procedure to weld small sections and let them cool before continuing, to prevent overheating.

entities.name: Increase weld speed
entities.description: Adjust welding travel speed to a faster rate to reduce heat input or defect formation.

entities.name: Coat of Slag Over Weld
entities.description: Observed condition where slag forms a thick, lumpy coating over the weld bead.

entities.name: Stick Weld – Crooked/Wavy Bead
entities.description: Observed irregular bead shape (topic listed but details not present on this page).

entities.name: Shielding
entities.description: Function of slag to protect the weld from impurities (shielding action described).

entities.name: previous bead
entities.description: Earlier deposited layer of weld metal adjacent to the current weld

entities.name: fill material
entities.description:
Additional weld material added to fill the joint
Material added to the weld (distinct from electrode wording) that must be clean and free of oil/coatings to prevent defects.

entities.name: adjust weld angle
aliases: adjust workpiece position or weld angle
entities.description: Change the angle of electrode or workpiece to permit welding to the bottom of the piece

entities.name: hold electrode at proper angle
entities.description: Maintain correct electrode and filler angles to control fusion and bead shape

entities.name: keep arc on leading edge
aliases: arc on leading edge of puddle
entities.description: Technique of positioning the arc at the front of the weld puddle for proper fusion

entities.name: increase amount of fill material
entities.description: Deposit more filler metal to eliminate gaps and ensure adequate weld volume

entities.name: decrease distance and increase bevel
entities.description: Reduce separation between workpieces and bevel edges to improve fit-up and fusion

entities.name: leading edge of weld puddle
entities.description: Front area of the molten weld puddle where the arc should be kept

entities.name: use two hands or rest hand
entities.description: Technique to steady the welder's hand (use two hands or rest on a steady surface) to reduce inaccurate welding.

Rear enclosure panel (listed twice)
